visual_attributes.morphology: aerial chariot resembling a bright cloud or a palace
distinctive_features: yoked with mule-like horses with ghost faces
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: multi-tiered structure with a central throne or seat

domain_specific_visual.aerial_design: resembles a palace or a house

functional_attributes.primary_function: transportation of divine beings and kings

domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: used by Ravana, Rama, and other mythological figures
domain_specific_functional.narrative_significance: central to the story of Ramayana

relationships.description: Ravana took the Pushpaka Vimana from Kubera
Rama returned the Pushpaka Vimana to Kubera after defeating Ravana
Originally made by Vishvakarma for Brahma

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: mythological regions of Lanka and Ayodhya
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents divine power and advanced technology in mythology
